tool.name: Sex.DetERRmine
tool.command:
## 1. Environment Setup ln -s '$input_bam' input.bam && samtools index input.bam &&
## 2. Extract Sample Name dynamically from BAM header SAMPLE_NAME=\$(samtools view -H input.bam | grep '^@RG' | sed 's/.*SM:\([^[:space:]]*\).*/\1/' | head -n 1) && if [ -z "\$SAMPLE_NAME" ]; then SAMPLE_NAME="Sample_1"; fi &&
## 3. BED Logic #if $input_bed cp '$input_bed' target.bed && #else printf "21	1	48129895
22	1	51304566
X	1	155270560
Y	1	59373566
" > target.bed && #end if
## 4. Generate depth and prep the file with the correct '#' header printf "#Chrom	Pos	\$SAMPLE_NAME
" > depth_final.txt && samtools depth -aa -q $base_quality -Q $map_quality -b target.bed input.bam >> depth_final.txt &&
## 5. Execute Sex.DetERRmine using the final formatted file sexdeterrmine -I depth_final.txt > '$output_report'

param.name: input_bed
param.label: Target Regions (BED) [Optional]
param.help: BED file defining the genomic regions to calculate coverage. Leave empty to use full-length chromosomes

param.name: base_quality
param.label: Base Quality (q)
param.help: Minimum base quality required to consider a read.

param.name: map_quality
param.label: Mapping Quality (Q)
param.help: Minimum mapping quality required to consider a read.

tool.help:
**Sex.DetERRmine: Sex Determination from Low-Coverage Sequencing Data**
This tool calculates the relative coverage of sex chromosomes (X and Y) compared to autosomes to determine the biological sex of a sample. It is particularly useful for ancient DNA (aDNA) and other low-depth sequencing data.
**How it works:**
1. **Coverage Calculation:** It uses `samtools depth` to calculate the per-base coverage across specified regions. 2. **Sex Rates:** It calculates two primary metrics: - **Rx (X-rate):** The ratio of X-chromosome coverage to autosomal coverage. - **Ry (Y-rate):** The ratio of Y-chromosome coverage to autosomal coverage.
**Default Behavior (Human-centric):**
.. note:: **If no Target Regions (BED) file is provided**, the tool will automatically use the full-length coordinates for human chromosomes **21, 22, X, and Y** (based on the hs37d5/hg19 reference). This matches the standard behavior of many bioinformatics pipelines (like nf-core/eager).
**Inputs:**
* **Input BAM file:** A coordinate-sorted and indexed BAM file. * **Target Regions (BED) [Optional]:** A BED file defining the regions to be analyzed. * **Quality Filters:** - **Base Quality (q):** Minimum base quality to include a base in depth calculation. - **Mapping Quality (Q):** Minimum mapping quality for a read to be considered.
**Outputs:**
A text report containing a table with the calculated Rx, Ry, and associated error values.
**Example Interpretation:**
========== ========== ==================== Metric Value Interpretation ---------- ---------- -------------------- Rx ~1.0 XX (Female) Ry ~0.0 XX (Female) Rx ~0.5 XY (Male) Ry >0.1 XY (Male) ========== ========== ====================
